title: Agricultural Engineer
description: An agricultural engineer is a professional who applies and uses the principles of agriculture and engineering to come up with solutions in the sector. They design and develop new farming technologies that help in making farming more efficient in terms of sustainability, safety and environment-friendly. Their roles entail designing, constructing and modernizing farming equipment and machinery to improve land use, increase yields, and conserve resources.
personality_traits: You are interested in science and technology
You like to solve practical problems
You care about the environment and sustainability
You enjoy working both indoors and outdoors
You pay attention to details while working
educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in the Science stream.
2. Complete Bachelor's degree in Agricultural
Engineering and Technology/Biotechnology.
Complete Bachelor's degree and follow it up with
Master's in Agriculture Engineering.
Complete Diploma in Agriculture Engineering.
For admissions, you must qualify JEE Main. JEE
Advanced is needed for admissions to IITs. Other
entrance exams can be taken:
ICAR/GATE/AIEEA/ MHT/CET/UPCATET/AP
EAMCET/CG PAT/SHIATS Entrance Exam/CCSHAU
Entrance Exam/Assam Agriculture University
VET/Rajasthan JET/AGRICET
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.

work_environment.work_environment: You will typically work in offices, but Engineer Trainee — Assistant Engineer — Senior may spend time ata variety of worksites, both indoors and Engineer > Operations Manager —> Head of outdoors. You may travel to agricultural settings to see that equipment and machinery are functioning according to the manufacturers’ specifications. Geographical Unit — Head of Company/CEO

example_from_field: Dr. R.K. Aggarwal is a renowned Agricultural Engineer who served with the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) for over three decades. He specialised in dryland agriculture and contributed to the development of improved farming techniques suited to the semi-arid regions of India.
source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 1, April 2025

title: Animal Health Worker
description: An Animal Health Worker provides preventive health care, assistance in disease control and basic first aid services to farm animals and poultry. He or she works under indirect or distant supervision of registered veterinarian.

title: Aquaculture Worker
personality_traits: You can determine the procedure of your own work
You pay attention to details
You are physically fit
You like to take care of animals
You would like to start your own business
You are good at planning and work according
required_stream: Complete 10+2 with Science stream - Biology as a
educational_pathway:
1. Minimum Class 10 pass or 10+2 in Science stream.
2. Complete a Certificate or Diploma course in Aquaculture, Fisheries Science, or Marine Biology.
OR Complete a B.F.Sc. (Bachelor of Fisheries Science) or B.Sc. in Aquaculture.
You may also enrol in NSQF-level vocational training in Aquaculture.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
career_growth_path: Aquaculture Helper → Aquaculture Worker → Aquaculture Supervisor → Aquaculture Manager → Head of Operations

work_environment.places_of_work: Fish farms, hatcheries, coastal aquaculture facilities, inland water bodies, seafood processing units.
work_environment.work_environment: It is mostly a field-based outdoor job involving work near water. Physical fitness is important. You may work 8-10 hours a day, 6 days a week. Seasonal demands can affect working hours.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can start your own aquaculture farm with government support under PMMSY (Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana).

example_from_field: Ratan Mondal from West Bengal started as an aquaculture worker in a local fish farm. Through the PMMSY scheme, he expanded to operate his own brackish water shrimp farm, earning over INR 6 lakh per year and employing five others from his village.
source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 1, April 2025

title: Dairy Farmer/Entrepreneur
personality_traits: You like to take care of animals
You would like to start your own business
You are good at planning and work according
required_stream: Complete 10+2 with Science stream - Biology as a
educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. Enrol in a Diploma or Certificate in Dairy Farming or Animal Husbandry.
OR Complete a B.Sc. in Dairy Science, Animal Husbandry or Veterinary Science.
NALCO, ICAR and state agricultural universities offer relevant programmes.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
career_growth_path: Farm Helper → Dairy Farmer → Dairy Farm Supervisor → Dairy Farm Manager → Dairy Entrepreneur/Owner

work_environment.places_of_work: Dairy farms, cooperative dairy societies, milk processing units, rural areas.
work_environment.work_environment: It is an outdoor, farm-based role requiring early morning work. Physical stamina is needed. Work is 7 days a week as animals require daily care.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can start your own dairy farm with support from NABARD and state dairy development boards. Cooperative membership with AMUL or similar cooperatives is also an option.

example_from_field: Shyamlal Pandey, a farmer from Uttar Pradesh, became a successful dairy entrepreneur after joining the Amul cooperative. By adopting improved breeds and scientific feeding practices, he expanded his farm from 5 to 25 cows, tripling his monthly income.
source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 1, April 2025

title: Fish and Sea Food Processor
personality_traits: You like to work in teams
You pay attention to details
You have good communication skills
educational_pathway:
1. Minimum Class 10 pass or 10+2.
2. Enrol in an NSQF level certificate course in Fish Processing or Food Processing.
OR Complete a Diploma or B.Sc. in Food Technology or Fisheries Science.
Central Institute of Fisheries Education (CIFE) and state fisheries colleges offer relevant courses.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
career_growth_path: Processing Helper → Fish Processor → Supervisor → Quality Inspector → Plant Manager

work_environment.places_of_work: Fish processing plants, seafood export units, canning factories, coastal processing facilities.
work_environment.work_environment: You work indoors in cold and wet processing environments. Physical stamina is required. Shift work is common. Hygiene and food safety protocols must be strictly followed.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can start a small-scale seafood processing unit with support from MPEDA (Marine Products Export Development Authority).

example_from_field: Meenal Krishnamurthy from Tamil Nadu trained through the Central Institute of Fisheries Technology (CIFT) and manages a mid-sized seafood export unit in Tuticorin that processes and exports frozen shrimp to markets in Japan and Europe.

example_from_field: Mr. Anil Sharma is a Fisheries Extension Officer in the Department of Fisheries, Government of Andhra Pradesh. He is known for his significant contributions to promoting sustainable fishing practices and improving fish farming techniques in rural areas. Mr. Sharma has worked extensively on educating local fishermen about modern aquaculture methods, enhancing their knowledge on water quality management, and introducing innovative fish feed technologies to improve yields. He has also been active in organizing training sessions for farmers and fishermen under various government schemes, including
source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 1, April 2025

title: Fishery Technician/Aquaculture Technician
personality_traits: You enjoy working near water or outdoors
You are patient and observant
You like to solve technical problems
You are comfortable in a team
You pay attention to details
required_stream: Minimum Class 10 pass. 10+2 in Science preferred.
educational_pathway:
1. Minimum Class 10 pass or 10+2 in Science.
2. Complete a Certificate or Diploma in Fisheries Technology or Aquaculture.
OR Complete B.F.Sc. (Bachelor of Fisheries Science).
Admission through ICAR-AIEEA or state fisheries university entrance exams.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
career_growth_path: Technician Trainee → Fishery Technician → Senior Technician → Fisheries Officer → Senior Fisheries Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: Fish hatcheries, aquaculture farms, fisheries research stations, fish seed production units.
work_environment.work_environment: You work both indoors in laboratories and outdoors at fish farms. Physical fitness is required. You may work 6 days a week, 8-9 hours a day. Field travel is part of the job.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can set up your own fish seed production or aquaculture consulting business.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in
Merit-based scholarships at institutes
loans: VidyaLakshmi portal: www.vidyalakshmi.co.in
Education loans from all banks
example_from_field: Arjun Das from Andhra Pradesh completed his B.F.Sc. from Andhra University and works as an Aquaculture Technician at a major shrimp hatchery in Nellore. He oversees larval production and water quality management.
source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 1, April 2025

title: Horticulturist
description: A Horticulturist’s primary role is the care, maintenance, cultivation propagation of plants. They apply their expertise to fruits, vegetables, ornamentals, and non-food crops to maximize their health or growth. They may also design landscapes or manage golf courses and sports turf.

You would like to start your own business

allied subject.

title: Hydroponics Technician
description: Pisciculturist Seed Technologist Shrimp Farmer Soil Scientist Tea Plantation Manager Vegetable Grower Veterinarian Vineyard Grower
personality_traits: You are interested in modern farming technology
You are detail oriented and analytical
You enjoy working in controlled environments
You like to experiment and innovate
You are comfortable using technology
required_stream: Complete 10+2 in Science stream (Physics, Chemistry and Biology).
educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science stream.
2. Complete a Certificate or Diploma in Hydroponics, Horticulture or Agriculture.
OR Complete a B.Sc. in Agriculture, Horticulture or Plant Science.
Several institutes and ICAR institutions offer specialised courses in protected cultivation and hydroponics.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
career_growth_path: Trainee Technician → Hydroponics Technician → Senior Technician → Hydroponics Farm Manager → Farm Director/Entrepreneur

work_environment.places_of_work: Hydroponic farms, greenhouse farms, urban farms, agricultural research centres, corporate farms.
work_environment.work_environment: You work in a controlled indoor environment such as greenhouses or growing tunnels. Work involves monitoring crops, maintaining water systems and nutrient management. You work 8-9 hours a day, 5-6 days a week.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can start your own hydroponic farm with support from the National Horticulture Mission and state agriculture departments.

source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 1, April 2025
